name: SMOTE and Image Augmentation for Imbalanced Scientific-Model Scoring
statement: Li, Haudek, and Krajcik used SMOTE and image augmentation to supplement imbalanced training data for automated scoring of student-generated scientific models, providing adjacent non-text constructed-response evidence.
population_or_context: High-school electroscope modeling responses scored across 13 analytic rubric categories.
quantitative_note: The article reports severe-category recall increasing from 0.52 to 0.89 after SMOTE, Category 11 F1 increasing from 0.47 to 0.86, and Category 8 recall increasing from 0.70 to 0.86 after SMOTE.
evaluation_method: CNN scoring models evaluated using tenfold cross-validation and independent testing with accuracy, precision, recall, and F1.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The authors report highly imbalanced rubric categories, including categories with positive cases below 10%, and note that high-performance responses can be scarce in complex assessment tasks.
synthetic_data_research_role: Training-set balancing and robustness/fairness analysis for underrepresented constructed-response categories.
reported_metrics: Accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 across no augmentation, general augmentation, and SMOTE conditions.
limitations: The synthetic data are image/feature-space augmentations, not generated textual student responses.
SMOTE was used to improve model training and fairness, not as independent validation of a deployed scorer.
source_locator: Full-text HTML; Abstract; Methods/Data Preparation; SMOTE implementations; Results/RQ2; Conclusions and Discussions.
